data.title: U.S. SEC Commissioner Releases "Crypto 2.0: Regulatory Spur": Coinbase Enforcement Actions Damage Regulatory Credibility
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Caroline A. Crenshaw, Commissioner of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, released "Crypto 2.0: Regulatory Whiplash", which pointed out that the enforcement action against Coinbase damaged the credibility of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's enforcement department, making people mistakenly believe that the law enforcement department will deploy enforcement resources based on election cycles or in a way that benefits those rich people, which has attracted criticism and sowed the seeds of distrust in the government. The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's responsibility is to do the right thing for investors, issuers, and capital markets.

data.title: Binance spokesperson: There is no massive selling of tokens, we are just helping users match transactions
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Social media had previously rumored that Binance was selling tokens during the market downturn, but Binance denied speculation about selling some of its assets and said it would not "dump" Solana and other tokens. A Binance spokesperson said: "Binance did not sell or sell tokens as claimed on social media. They misunderstood the role of Binance as an exchange. It only helps users match transactions. It is important not to jump to conclusions based on transaction screenshots." (Cointelegraph)

data.description: According to Odaily, Bitcoin has experienced a rebound, breaking through the 81,000 USDT mark. It is currently trading at 81,010 USDT. The 24-hour decline has narrowed to 6.09%.

data.title: Safe announces resumption of online operations on the Arbitrum network
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that the cryptocurrency wallet Safe{Wallet} announced that it has resumed online on the Arbitrum network. Its team will restore access to more networks, giving priority to the networks that are most active and have the most direct impact on users. More networks will soon resume support.

data.title: Greeks.live: Traders are eyeing $75,000 and $67,000-70,000 as potential support ranges
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Greeks.live macro researcher Adam released an English community briefing, which revealed that in the week when Bitcoin plummeted from $94,000-96,000 to about $80,000, the group generally held a bearish attitude, and this sharp sell-off caused community members to suffer heavy losses. Traders are watching $75,000 and $67,000-70,000 as potential support ranges, and there is some debate as to whether this has bottomed out or may fall further. Many traders have experienced their biggest losses ever, with some reporting a 70% retracement of their accounts from all-time highs and "7 months of profits wiped out". The sell-off has been described as having runaway momentum, with no clear catalyst, and even surprised experienced traders who held high confidence in the $94,000-96,000 range. Some traders partially saved their positions through risk management with protective put options, while others sought to implement a put spread strategy (selling a $65,000 put option expiring in April and buying a $70,000 put option) to recover losses.

data.title: Bitget will cooperate with UNHCR and others to donate 100,000 Iftar meals
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bitget announced that it will cooperate with well-known humanitarian organizations such as the UN Refugee Agency and the UN World Food Programme during Ramadan to donate 100,000 Iftar meals to groups in need, aiming to help vulnerable communities facing major crises alleviate hunger.
According to the plan, Bitget will first donate 10,000 Iftar meals. At the same time, during Ramadan, Bitget will hold a series of Iftar dinners in Dubai, Istanbul and other places, and invite guests participating in the dinner to participate in fundraising. Bitget will match their kindness 1:1 and donate the same amount. In addition, through exclusive cooperation with La Liga, Bitget will also hold a charity auction at the dinner, and all auction proceeds will be used for this charity event until 100,000 Iftar meals are donated. Vugar Usi Zade, COO of Bitget, said: "During Ramadan, through our global network, we hope to bring practical impact to communities facing difficulties, demonstrate the responsibility and responsibility of the crypto industry, and help more people get through the difficulties."

data.title: Degen: The 13th season of Airdrop 2 will be collected on March 5
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Degen announced on the X platform that the Airdrop 2 Season 13 collection date is scheduled for March 5.
